title: Chapter Nineteen: Recommendation
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Truly remarkable." Ma Yeon-jeok circled him with quick, eager steps. "An unbelievable level. At that age."

His wrinkled face showed undisguised amazement. The handsome boy beside him was visibly displeased.

Yeon-shin bowed his head slightly. Hands clasped.

"Greetings to my grandfather. I am Radiant Demon Wing's Jeong Yeon-shin."

Silence.

The word *grandfather* landed in the room like a stone dropped into still water. Ma Yeon-jeok's spotless beard trembled.

"You are..."

He was a purple-rank master who had looked down on the world from the pinnacle of the Divine Sword Squad. The intuition of such men was said to be terrifying.

He looked at Yeon-shin. He looked at the Sect Leader, who wore a faint smile.

Understanding broke across his face.

"Jeong from the Jeong family. Patriarch Jeong."

"It is our first meeting," Yeon-shin said.

His mother's people. Never came to Xinye County. Not for the wedding. Not after his mother died giving birth to him. Ties severed with a daughter who married into a third-rate family.

The door inside him rattled. He kept it shut.

"You're my sister's son?" Ma Jin's voice, thick with shock.

*Father and son, then. Ma Yeon-jeok and Ma Jin. The same surname.*

"You mentioned a master," Yeon-shin said, his voice level, "but you're my uncle? I don't know my maternal family tree. I was a cast-off child from my extinct paternal family."

He had heard the conversation through the door before entering. His senses, sharpening toward the level of a master, had caught every word.

*How far could the blood of a trivial estate go? It's fortunate if he doesn't become a disgrace to the Desolate Ma family.*

Ma Yeon-jeok's own words. About him.

"I understand. Desolate Sect, Black rank. You wouldn't have had time to visit a house with no sister."

The pointed words landed. Ma Jin flinched. Ma Yeon-jeok's face darkened.

*Thanks to my mother's family, I survived the Blade Sect Leader's hand. That is enough.*

"Leader. May I have a private word with that child?"

"I am the child of an outsider," Yeon-shin said before the Sect Leader could answer. "As a Desolate martial artist, I respect the achievements of my maternal grandfather. But further private conversation would be burdensome."

*Outsider.* The term for a married daughter who no longer belonged to her birth family. How they had treated his mother.

"I cannot accept this!" The handsome boy surged forward. "How dare he be so disrespectful to the former Divine Sword Squad leader?"

"Your acceptance isn't needed. I'm not part of the Desolate Ma family. I'm Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Flash."

"How dare--"

"If you're asking for a lesson, I can oblige. But I have no obligation to prove my worth to you or your family."

The energy of the Radiant Demon Formation bled from him -- not deliberately released, but not restrained. The killing edge of a warrior who had slaughtered a cult branch alone, pressing outward through his white robes.

The boy reached for his hand-blades.

*He's mainly trained in fist techniques.*

Yeon-shin withdrew his aura. Completely. Instantly. The control was its own statement.

"Please forgive my rudeness," he said to the Sect Leader. "I showed an unrestrained attitude."

She smiled.

"That's enough arguing. Lightning Flash is a promising subordinate, and Yeon-juk is an old friend. Their conflict is troublesome, so it is best to resolve it as martial artists."

She looked at Yeon-shin. The smile deepened.

"I know there is no benefit for you. I'll grant you one request, regardless of the outcome."

"Even that?"

He meant the fruit.

"That is still difficult. However, I can teach you one of my signature techniques."

*Martial arts from the Sect Leader. A peerless master close to the world's finest.*

Greed flashed across the handsome boy's face.

"I'll do it," Yeon-shin said.

---

The Sect Leader's training ground was marble. White stone, veined with pale blue. Precious weapons on stone racks. Worth more than everything the Jeong family had ever owned.

She stood at the edge. Light green windbreaker stirring in the spring breeze.

Yeon-shin saw her sword.

He had not noticed it before. He had perceived it as part of nature -- not a weapon but a presence, like the tree in her chamber or the wind against the walls. The realisation of what that meant struck him cold.

*Beyond sword-body unification. Something else entirely. Chilling.*

Her eyes caught his.

"You have good eyes."

"I apologise."

The handsome boy stood at the centre of the marble with black hand-blades on both fists. Elegant equipment for the heir of the Desolate Ma family.

"Did you get distracted by the lord's grace?" the boy sneered. "You lowly creature."

"Aren't you from a branch family?"

"How dare--"

Yeon-shin closed his eyes.

*I can obtain the fruit of the World Tree on my own. The Blood Master Sword proved it.*

*Radiant Demon Wing is enough.*

He opened his eyes.

"Come."

The boy charged. The Desolate Ma family's footwork -- honed over generations, aggressive, low. The fist that came in was wrapped in spiralling energy, heavier than anything Yeon-shin had felt from an opponent his own age.

He deflected with the back of his hand. The impact jolted through his wrist. He braced his elbow, feeding strength from the Dynamic Technique.

*His energy surpasses mine. Elixirs. But energy isn't everything.*

He applied the time-force principle. His own qi layered at the critical moment. His hands, following the Radiant Demon Formation, began to dismantle the boy's technique piece by piece.

The Desolate Ma fist was linear. Heavy. Each strike committed. Full power into one fist, then a sudden burst from the other. Various principles harmoniously mixed.

*So this is what true strong fists are like.*

Despite the boy's temperament, the technique was excellent. Exquisite movements appeared between the rage. The kind of martial arts that could only be produced by centuries of practice.

Enough to plant ideas.

"Why don't you draw your sword!"

"Because your fist technique is excellent."

He said it while spinning like a leaf, breaking an elbow strike. He was reverse-engineering -- reading energy patterns, working backward from result to principle.

The marble cracked under the boy's stamp. A fist came with full bodyweight, wind screaming along the knuckles. Yeon-shin deflected it and landed.

"I've thought of a strong fist technique," he said. "Ten forms should do."

Blue-green light flickered in his eyes.

At the edge, three masters watched.

"Ten forms from there?" Ma Yeon-jeok muttered.

"No," Ma Jin said, his voice strained. "It doesn't mean he'll end it in ten moves. It means he'll create ten forms in that time."

---
